blindspots: **Organic vs. inorganic revenue split** — SPSC doesn't cleanly disclose. Core EDI growth rate is inferred, not stated. Proxy: recurring revenue growth ex-acquisition cohorts.

**MAX AI adoption rate** — No public metrics on MAX penetration across 54,600 customers. $2K-5K/year per customer = $100-250M incremental ARR at scale. Monitor via: earnings call commentary on AI attach rates.
**1P/3P seller breakdown for recovery segment** — Amazon/Walmart seller dynamics drive recovery revenue. No reliable external tracking of SPSC's specific client mix.
**ERP vendor AI roadmap (SAP S/4HANA, Oracle)** — These are building AI-native supply chain modules. If they internalize EDI, SPSC's moat weakens. Monitor via: SAP/Oracle product announcements.

cyclical.context: Tracks upsell + MAX AI adoption. Slow and steady expected
cyclical.name: ARPU trend

cyclical.context: At $55, buyback highly accretive. Pace signals management confidence
cyclical.name: Buyback activity

cyclical.context: Monitor for further headwinds or normalization. Platform risk is structural for recovery segment
cyclical.name: Amazon/Walmart platform policy

noiseFilter: **Headline revenue growth rate (7%)** — Masks core EDI growing ~20%. Headline is depressed by acquisition segment drag. Track core and recovery separately.
**C-suite departures** — 4 in 12 months is concerning but partly driven by activist pressure + acquisition integration. Judge by operational metrics, not personnel news.
**Daily share price volatility** — $55 is near multi-year trough. Low-volume swings are amplified.
**Analyst PT revisions without new modeling** — Most estimates don't separate core vs. recovery. Only react to analysts who model segment-level dynamics.

structural.name: Core EDI fulfillment growth (YoY)
structural.threshold: <15% = moat weakening; <10% = structural threat from ERP vendors; >20% = healthy

structural.current: -350 net (Q4 2025; entirely 3P Carbon6 by design). Organic 1P ~1,100 through Q1-Q3 2025 per Analyst Day; Q4 2025 1P flat sequentially (enablement timing)
structural.name: Net customer adds — **Q1 2026 organic 1P adds ⭐ PIVOTAL**
structural.threshold: **Q1 2026 1P adds ≥1,000 annualized = TRANSIENT-by-design confirmed (thesis intact); <800 annualized = STRUCTURAL churn → triggers U1 Part E cap → pattern-match flips to PASS.** Negative 2+ consecutive total quarters in 1P = structural churn signal

structural.name: Revenue recovery segment trajectory
structural.threshold: Stabilization = trough confirmed; continued decline = SupplyPike/Carbon6 impaired

structural.name: EBITDA margin
structural.threshold: <28% = cost structure concern; >33% = operating leverage thesis validated

structural.current: Anson Funds pushing for CEO change/sale; Irenic Capital Q4 2025 position; 4 independent directors added
structural.name: Activist campaign progress
structural.threshold: Proxy contest filed = near-term catalyst; settlement = moderate catalyst; activist exits = negative signal

structural.current: Historical: CEO Collins consistent net SELLER across all price points ($170→$147→$59, all 10b5-1). CFO Nelson (outgoing) also seller. Zero open-market purchases by any insider. Total insider ownership ~1.6%. Pattern-match A2 scored 2/5.
structural.name: **Insider purchase activity ⭐ WATCH MANDATE**
structural.threshold: **Any open-market insider PURCHASE at/near $52.55 trough = material signal; cluster buying (≥2 insiders) → upgrades A2 from 2 to 4-5/5 and re-opens conviction assessment. Continued selling at trough = bearish confirmation; stop of selling = neutral.** Monitor: SEC Form 4 filings; secform4.com aggregator

trendLog.action: Watch — not yet confirmed structural. Q1 2026 earnings (~May) is resolution point
trendLog.assessment: **First negative quarter.** Could be acquisition integration noise or structural churn. Must monitor Q1 2026 for confirmation.

trendLog.action: No structural breach. Net customer adds (#2) still awaiting Q1 2026 confirmation (~May). MAX AI adoption is next key catalyst.
trendLog.assessment: Core EDI growth ~20% confirmed (hidden by headline). EBITDA 31% within threshold range (28-33%). MAX AI in beta — no adoption metrics yet. CFO transition is planned succession. 7% headline guidance masks segment dynamics.
trendLog.new: FY2025: Rev $751.5M (+18%), recurring +20%, fulfillment +22%, EBITDA $231.4M (+24%, 31% margin), ~54,600 customers, ARPU ~$14,350 (+5-6%). **100th consecutive quarter of revenue growth.** Buyback expanded +$200M to $300M total (76% FCF returned). MAX AI launched in beta Q4 2025. CFO Kim Nelson retiring; Joseph Del Preto named (Mar 2026). FY2026 guide: ~7% rev growth, +2pp EBITDA margin. No Anson/Irenic updates.

trendLog.action: Monitor Q1 2026 earnings (~May); monitor SEC Form 4 filings continuously via `edgar-monitor.sh SPSC`. Scorecard file: `_Screening/CapIQ Screeners/2026-04-15_SPSC_pattern_match.md`
trendLog.assessment: Indicator #2 tightened with specific Q1 2026 threshold; new indicator #6 (Insider Purchase Activity) added as STRUCTURAL watch mandate. Both gate-level inputs to any future conviction reassessment.
trendLog.new: Entry price alert fired ($52.55 < $55 threshold). Pattern-match re-run under Mixed F+A archetype: 29.5/35 STRONG, U1=5 (Override C Interesting-High floor fired, upgraded to Strong via total-score). RC 2/5 blocks WATCHLIST conviction upgrade per Rule 6. User watch mandate: (a) **Q1 2026 organic 1P customer adds** — pivotal input; <800 annualized fires U1 Part E cap and flips scorecard to PASS. (b) **Insider purchase activity** — any open-market buy at/near trough is a material signal; cluster buying upgrades A2 from 2 → 4-5.
